description: Side-by-side diptych comparing the two states of an optical switch beam-interrupt sensor. Left panel: stem at rest, beam-window slot aligned to the LED/phototransistor optical axis — beam passes through, phototransistor receives signal, state reads HIGH. Right panel: stem actuated (pressed down), solid stem body crosses the optical axis, beam is blocked before reaching the phototransistor — signal drops LOW and the firmware registers a keypress. Terracotta beam line in both panels; blocking cross drawn at stem face in actuated state; PT shown dim when not receiving. Monospace labels identify components and output state.

data_sources.content: article brief and thock bearings.md style guide
